key_highlights: S&P 500 futures rise on strong Big Tech earnings (Alphabet, Amazon) but tech stocks slide on OpenAI missing targets.
Federal Reserve holds rates steady for third straight time amid Iran war-driven inflation; Powell stays on Fed board until DOJ probe ends.
Oil prices surge to 4-year highs due to Iran conflict and Hormuz Strait blockade; UAE quits OPEC, adding geopolitical risk.
Japan intervenes in FX markets to support yen, triggering gold rebound and dollar weakness.
AI spending fatigue emerges as Meta sells $25B debt and investors question ROI; AI hardware split between Nvidia and TSMC.
Gas prices hit $4.30/gallon national average, pressuring consumers and airlines (Air Canada pulls 2026 outlook).
Bitcoin capped by $82K sell wall amid risk-off from UAE OPEC exit and Iran tensions.
Jobless claims fall to 189,000, lowest in 5 decades, signaling labor market strength.
Emerging markets see best month since 2022 on AI boom but oil fears persist.
Regulatory actions: FCC directs Disney TV stations to file early license renewals; bipartisan bill to ban Chinese vehicles.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Big Tech earnings (Alphabet, Amazon) beat estimates on AI cloud demand, but OpenAI missing targets and Rambus post-earnings plunge signal AI hype fatigue. Meta sells $25B debt amid investor worry.
sectors.implications: AI capex spending is under scrutiny; investors demand ROI. TSMC doubling down on AI (A13) vs Nvidia split. Microsoft Azure growth outlook strong but headcount to decrease. May 7 Microsoft event key.

sectors.trend: Oil at wartime highs (4-year high) due to Iran conflict, Hormuz Strait blockade, UAE OPEC exit. Petrobras output record. Gas prices $4.30/gallon.
sectors.implications: Inflation fears rising; Fed stuck. Airlines (Air Canada) pull outlook. Chevron stock under analyst review. Mercuria sues Baltic Exchange over benchmark. White House briefed on Iran options.

sectors.trend: Robinhood sinks on crypto revenue plunge; SoFi falls on mixed earnings. Bitcoin capped by $82K sell wall. MoonPay acquires Sodot for institutional digital assets. Hong Kong sees fake stablecoins rise. CFTC reviews Kalshi expansion.
sectors.implications: Crypto winter continues for retail-focused firms; institutional adoption (MoonPay, Securitize) grows. Regulatory scrutiny on stablecoins and prediction markets.

sectors.trend: Tanger beats earnings on Gen Z return to physical stores. Uber adds hotel bookings to become travel one-stop shop. Walmart analyst views positive. Gas prices squeeze consumer spending.
sectors.implications: Physical retail resilient; travel demand strong but jet fuel costs rising. Consumer staples (Kroger downgraded to Hold) face margin pressure.

sectors.trend: Hemab (Novo-backed) raises $301.5M in upsized IPO; Seaport Therapeutics raises $255M in IPO. Biotech IPOs active.
sectors.implications: Biotech funding environment improving; IPO market showing signs of life beyond tech.

sectors.trend: General Dynamics Q1 earnings beat. Defense spending elevated amid Iran conflict. S&P weighs new index rules for mega IPOs.
sectors.implications: Defense stocks benefit from geopolitical tensions; infrastructure and industrial demand steady.

sectors.trend: Best month since 2022 on AI boom but oil fear caps gains. India heat exposes fragile grid; energy crunch deepens. Australia home prices slowest since early 2025.
sectors.implications: EM equities attractive but energy shock and currency volatility (yen intervention) create headwinds. India energy crisis a risk.

sectors.trend: Australia home prices slow; Montana couple faces 107% property tax spike. Homeowners insurance water damage queries rise.
sectors.implications: Housing affordability worsening; property tax burden rising in some regions. Insurance costs up.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: Iran war, Hormuz blockade, UAE OPEC exit, oil at 4-year high.
AI capex fatigue vs. strong earnings: Big Tech beats but spending sprees questioned.
Fed on hold: Rates steady, inflation gauge at 2-year high, Powell stays on board.
Currency intervention: Japan yen surges, dollar weakens, gold rebounds.
Consumer strain: Gas prices $4.30, airlines pull outlook, jobless claims low but real wages squeezed.
Regulatory crosswinds: FCC on Disney, Chinese vehicle ban, stablecoin scrutiny.
IPO market revival: Biotech IPOs upsized, S&P considers faster mega IPO inclusion.
Crypto bifurcation: Retail crypto revenue plunges (Robinhood), institutional infrastructure builds (MoonPay, Securitize).

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase energy sector exposure (XLE, CVX, XOM); hedge with gold (GLD) and inflation-protected bonds (TIP). Reduce airline and consumer discretionary exposure.
Diversify within tech: favor companies with proven AI revenue (MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL) over pure-play hardware (NVDA, TSM) if spending slows. Monitor Meta debt sale as sentiment indicator.
Hold short-duration bonds, TIPS, and commodities. Avoid long-duration fixed income. Favor value stocks over growth. Monitor Fed rhetoric and inflation data.
Hedge USD/JPY exposure via futures or options. Gold benefits from weaker dollar. Japanese equities may face headwinds from stronger yen.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer Spending Slowdown from High Gas Prices
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to discretionary retail, restaurants, and airlines. Favor discount retailers (WMT, TGT) and essential consumer staples. Monitor jobless claims (currently low).
Avoid Disney (DIS) on FCC license risk; monitor Chinese auto ban (short LI, NIO, XPEV). Crypto: favor regulated infrastructure plays (COIN, MSTR) over retail brokers (HOOD).
Selective participation: favor biotech IPOs with strong backers (Hemab, Seaport). S&P rule change could accelerate mega IPO inclusion, benefiting index funds.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Alphabet (GOOGL) on strong cloud AI revenue and record market cap jump
opportunities.rationale: Alphabet sales beat on Google Cloud and AI customers; second biggest one-day market cap jump ever. AI monetization is real and accelerating.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate gold (GLD) as hedge against geopolitical risk and dollar weakness
opportunities.rationale: Gold rebounds on yen intervention and dollar weakness; Iran conflict supports safe-haven demand. Fed on hold limits real rate headwinds.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy TSMC (TSM) on AI hardware leadership and A13 ramp
opportunities.rationale: TSMC doubling down on AI with A13; earnings reveal AI hardware power split with Nvidia. Strong secular demand from cloud and edge AI.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy infrastructure (XLE, CVX) on oil supply disruption
opportunities.rationale: Oil at 4-year high, Iran blockade, UAE OPEC exit. Petrobras output record. Energy sector benefits from sustained high prices and underinvestment.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Amazon (AMZN) on cloud AI strength and travel expansion
opportunities.rationale: Amazon tops cloud expectations on strong AI demand; shares rise. Uber adds hotel bookings, but Amazon's AWS AI growth is more durable.

opportunities.recommendation: Selectively participate in biotech IPOs (Hemab, Seaport)
opportunities.rationale: Upsized IPOs indicate strong investor appetite for innovative biotech. Novo-backed Hemab and Seaport have strong pipelines.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to airlines and consumer discretionary
defensive_moves.rationale: Air Canada pulls 2026 outlook on rising jet fuel costs; gas prices at $4.30 squeeze consumer budgets. Avis plunges on wider loss.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge tech portfolio with put options on AI hardware names (NVDA, TSM)
defensive_moves.rationale: AI capex fatigue and OpenAI missing targets could trigger correction. Rambus post-earnings plunge is a warning. Use protective puts.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to short-duration Treasuries and TIPS
defensive_moves.rationale: Fed on hold, inflation gauge at 2-year high. Protect against stagflation risk. TIPS provide inflation hedge.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Sell or reduce Robinhood (HOOD) on crypto revenue plunge
defensive_moves.rationale: Earnings hit by plunge in crypto revenue; retail trading slowdown. Regulatory scrutiny on stablecoins adds risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Disney (DIS) on FCC license renewal risk
defensive_moves.rationale: FCC directs Disney-owned TV stations to file early license renewals; Republican AGs join lawsuit against local TV merger. Regulatory overhang.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets remain volatile as geopolitical risk (Iran, oil) clashes with strong earnings (Big Tech). S&P 500 near record but energy shock and AI spending fatigue could trigger a 5-10% correction. Fed on hold limits downside but inflation fears cap upside. Yen intervention adds FX uncertainty. Defensive positioning (energy, gold, short-duration bonds) recommended.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: AI-driven productivity gains (cloud, enterprise) support structural growth, but energy inflation and geopolitical fragmentation could slow global GDP. Fed may need to cut if economy weakens, but Iran war keeps oil high. Emerging markets benefit from AI boom but face energy headwinds. IPO market revival (biotech, tech) positive for capital formation. Watch for Fed transition to Warsh and DOJ probe outcome.
key_catalysts: Iran war escalation or de-escalation (Hormuz Strait reopening)
Big Tech earnings (Microsoft May 7 event, Nvidia, Meta)
Fed policy meeting minutes and Powell's testimony
Japan FX intervention continuation and BOJ policy
US CPI and PCE inflation data (May)
OPEC+ response to UAE exit and Iran supply disruption
AI spending announcements from hyperscalers (MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, META)
DOJ probe into Fed and Powell's future role
S&P index rule changes for mega IPOs
